assumptions: Traffic assumed ~<50k uploads/month (no intake answer provided) — Lambda + DynamoDB on-demand is right-sized for this volume
Downtime tolerance assumed moderate — single-AZ budget, multi-AZ balanced, cross-region resilient
No compliance regime stated — standard AWS security baseline applied
Videos assumed up to ~5GB; S3 multipart upload used for large files
Notification channel assumed email (SES); Slack/PagerDuty added at resilient tier
Transcoded output stored in S3; input and output buckets are separate for least-privilege scoping
MediaConvert chosen as managed transcoder — no self-hosted FFmpeg needed at any tier
Cost estimates are on-demand list prices for us-east-1, presented as ranges over assumed monthly volumes. They exclude the AWS Free Tier, Savings Plans, Reserved Instances, and negotiated discounts.
Assumed traffic ~100k–1M requests/month, applied equally to all three tiers (tiers differ by robustness, not traffic). Set "expected monthly visitors" in intake to change it.

tiers.name: budget
tiers.summary: Serverless-no-VPC: pre-signed S3 upload → SQS-buffered MediaConvert dispatch → DynamoDB job tracking → SES notification. Zero idle cost; scales to concurrent jobs without fleet management.

security: KMS key access scoped to input bucket

edges.payload: CreateJob request (input S3 URI, output preset)
metric filters (error rate, DLQ depth, duration)

delta: Baseline: fully serverless-no-VPC — zero always-on compute cost
Pre-signed URL pattern keeps binary video out of Lambda/API Gateway data path
SQS ingest buffer + DLQ handles upload burst without dropping events
DynamoDB on-demand absorbs parallel completion write spikes
MediaConvert per-minute billing — zero idle transcoder cost
SES outbox pattern: delivery record written before send, flipped on ack
Single-AZ implicit (Lambda/DynamoDB/SQS are regional; S3 is always 3-AZ)
CloudWatch alarms on: DLQ depth, Lambda error rate, MediaConvert job failures → SNS email
tradeoffs: No reserved Lambda concurrency provisioning — cold starts possible under sudden burst
No multi-region DR — regional service outage affects all tiers of the stack
SES delivery retries are eventual — notification may lag minutes under bounce/retry
DLQ messages require manual redrive or monitoring action to recover
Single ops SNS topic emails only — no on-call escalation path

costDrivers.note: Data transfer out to internet, first 10TB/month tier (North America). First 1TB/month is free.
HTTPS requests ($0.0075 per 10,000). North America/Europe.
Compute duration: $0.0000166667 per GB-second (x86). Multiply by memoryGB x durationSeconds per invocation.
On-demand list price ($0.20 per 1M requests). Excludes Free Tier.
S3 Standard storage, first 50TB/month tier.
PUT/COPY/POST/LIST requests per 1,000. GET/SELECT requests are ~$0.0004 per 1,000.
Standard queue requests ($0.40 per 1M). First 1M/month free. FIFO is $0.50 per 1M.
On-demand read request units ($0.125 per 1M RRU). 1 RRU = 1 strongly-consistent read up to 4KB.
On-demand write request units ($0.625 per 1M WRU). 1 WRU = 1 write up to 1KB.
Email send: $0.10 per 1,000 emails (content). Attachment/data transfer billed separately.
CloudWatch Logs ingestion $0.50/GB (first 10GB/mo free in some accounts). Archival storage is cheaper; Insights queries extra.
Standard topic API requests ($0.50 per 1M). Delivery (HTTP/SMS/email) priced separately.
X-Ray traces: $5.00 per 1M traces recorded (first 100k/mo free).

tiers.name: balanced
tiers.summary: Adds Lambda provisioned concurrency, SQS FIFO option for ordered jobs, CloudWatch dashboards + X-Ray tracing on all paths, Slack alerting on critical alarms, and explicit MediaConvert queue prioritization. Still single-region; all services remain serverless-no-VPC.

security: KMS key access scoped to input bucket

edges.payload: CreateJob request (input S3 URI, output preset)
metric filters (error rate, DLQ depth, duration)

delta: Lambda provisioned concurrency (5) on dispatch + completion — eliminates cold-start burst lag
MediaConvert priority queue added — separates premium/standard job lanes
CloudWatch Dashboard added — golden signals (error rate, queue depth, job duration) visible in one pane
Slack alerting via SNS → Lambda forwarder — critical alarms reach on-call channel instantly
X-Ray tracing enabled across full async path (presign → dispatch → completion)
tradeoffs: Provisioned concurrency adds a small always-on Lambda cost even at zero traffic
Still single-region — no failover if a regional AWS service degrades
MediaConvert priority queue requires job classification logic in dispatch_fn
Slack webhook URL is a secret requiring rotation discipline

tiers.name: resilient
tiers.summary: Adds cross-region active-passive DR: S3 Cross-Region Replication to a DR bucket, DynamoDB Global Tables for job/notify state, EventBridge cross-region event routing, Route 53 health-check failover, and PagerDuty escalation on SLO alarms.

security: KMS key access scoped to input bucket
health checks on API GW endpoints

edges.payload: CreateJob request (input S3 URI, output preset)
metric filters (error rate, DLQ depth, duration)

delta: S3 CRR replicates input + output buckets to DR region — RPO ~15 min for video assets
DynamoDB Global Tables (2-region) — job + delivery state survives primary-region failure
EventBridge cross-region bus routes MediaConvert completion events to DR Lambda — RTO <5 min
Route 53 health-check failover — DNS cuts over to DR API Gateway on primary health failure
SLO composite alarm + anomaly-detection alarm added — proactive before user impact
PagerDuty escalation via SNS → Lambda — on-call paged with incident severity + runbook link
EventBridge archive + replay enabled — replay events after DR recovery without data loss
tradeoffs: Global Tables + CRR + EventBridge cross-region add meaningful always-on replication cost
Active-passive DR: DR region is cold — warm-up of Lambda provisioned concurrency takes ~1 min on failover
Cross-region MediaConvert jobs must be re-submitted in DR region for in-flight jobs at failure time
Complexity of two-region state synchronization requires disciplined DR runbook and regular failover drills

keyDecisions.rationale: MediaConvert is fully managed, scales to concurrent jobs without fleet management, and pays per minute of output — zero idle cost, aligning with cost-optimization and operational-excellence pillars.

keyDecisions.chosen: S3 pre-signed URL (direct client → S3 upload, no proxy)
alternativesConsidered: Proxy upload through API Gateway + Lambda
keyDecisions.rationale: Pre-signed URLs offload binary transfer to S3, keeping Lambda/API Gateway outside the data path and eliminating the 10 MB Lambda payload limit — best for performance efficiency and cost.

keyDecisions.chosen: S3 Event Notification → SQS → Lambda worker
keyDecisions.rationale: SQS buffers upload spikes and decouples ingest from transcoder capacity, giving at-least-once delivery with DLQ safety — reliability pillar over the simpler but drop-prone direct Lambda invocation.

keyDecisions.chosen: SES with delivery-status event publishing to DynamoDB job record
keyDecisions.rationale: SES gives observable, retryable delivery with bounce/complaint tracking; flipping the job record to 'delivered' on success satisfies the outbox pattern for reliable notification — bare SNS email lacks per-message ack.

DynamoDB on-demand absorbs write spikes from parallel transcode completions with no provisioned capacity, and its conditional-write gives idempotent job-status updates — cost-optimization + reliability at zero idle cost.

securityFloor: Encrypt all data at rest (KMS / service-native SSE), preferring customer-managed keys.
Enforce TLS in transit everywhere; terminate HTTPS at the edge and reject plaintext.
Least-privilege IAM: scoped per-service roles, no wildcards, no long-lived access keys.
Enable S3 Block Public Access account-wide so no bucket or object can be made public.
Keep the data tier private: VPC-bound stores in private subnets with no public route; managed/serverless stores (DynamoDB/S3) are already private via IAM and need no VPC or NAT.
Store secrets in AWS Secrets Manager / SSM Parameter Store — never in code or images.
Front public endpoints with CloudFront + AWS WAF (managed + rate-based rules); Shield Standard for L3/L4.
Enable CloudTrail plus S3/CloudFront/ALB access logs and VPC Flow Logs for audit.
